title: DatabaseCartographySection
description: Database cartography output section of the memory_cartographer envelope. Contains the results of a database schema scan: data sources, tables, columns, constraints, relations, migration lineage, and query touchpoints. See docs/contracts/memory-cartographer-contract.md and normalization-rules.md for ordering and stability guarantees.

partial.description: true if this section represents incomplete results (e.g., due to timeout, connection failure, or cap reached). When true, the top-level generation_metadata.partial is also true.

datasources.description: Data source descriptors for each database connection discovered in the workspace. Ordered by the datasource identity key ascending (see normalization-rules.md).

tables.description: Table (and view) descriptors across all scanned data sources. Ordered by: (1) datasource_id ascending, then (2) schema_name ascending (empty schema sorts before non-empty), then (3) table_name ascending.

columns.description: Column descriptors for all tables. Ordered by: (1) table_id ascending, then (2) ordinal_position ascending.

constraints.description: Constraint descriptors (primary keys, unique, check, not-null). Ordered by: (1) table_id ascending, then (2) constraint_kind ascending, then (3) constraint_name ascending.

relations.description: Foreign key and logical relations between tables. Ordered by: (1) from_table_id ascending, then (2) to_table_id ascending, then (3) constraint_name ascending.

migration_lineage.description: Migration file tracking and schema evolution lineage discovered in the workspace.

query_touchpoints.description: Source code locations that reference database tables or columns (raw SQL, ORM queries). Ordered by: (1) file ascending, then (2) line ascending.

DataSourceEntry.title: DataSourceEntry
DataSourceEntry.description: A database connection or data source discovered in the workspace.

id.description: Stable identity key for this data source. Format: '{kind}::{name}' (workspace-scoped). See normalization-rules.md.

name.description: Human-readable name for the data source (e.g., database name or connection alias).

config_path.description: Workspace-relative path to the configuration file where this data source was discovered, when applicable.

TableEntry.title: TableEntry
TableEntry.description: A table or view in a scanned data source.

id.description: Stable identity key. Format: '{datasource_id}::{schema_name}::{table_name}' (schema_name may be empty for databases without schema namespacing). See normalization-rules.md.

datasource_id.description: Identity key of the parent DataSourceEntry.

schema_name.description: Database schema/namespace containing this table (e.g., 'public', 'dbo'). Empty string when the database has no schema namespacing.

table_name.description: Table or view name as it appears in the database.

column_count.description: Number of columns in this table.

row_count_estimate.description: Estimated row count from database statistics, when available. Absent when not available.

ColumnEntry.title: ColumnEntry
ColumnEntry.description: A single column in a table.

id.description: Stable identity key. Format: '{table_id}::{column_name}'. See normalization-rules.md.

table_id.description: Identity key of the parent TableEntry.

column_name.description: Column name as it appears in the database.

ordinal_position.description: 1-based ordinal position of the column in the table definition. Primary sort key within a table's columns.

nullable.description: true if the column allows NULL values. Always present (not optional for columns — see normalization-rules.md).

default_value.description: Default value expression as a string, when present. Absent when no default is defined.

is_primary_key.description: true if this column is part of the primary key.

ConstraintEntry.title: ConstraintEntry
ConstraintEntry.description: A table constraint (primary key, unique, check, not-null).

table_id.description: Identity key of the parent TableEntry.

constraint_name.description: Constraint name as it appears in the database.

column_ids.description: Ordered list of column identity keys participating in this constraint.

check_expression.description: Check constraint expression as a string. Present only for check constraints.

RelationEntry.title: RelationEntry
RelationEntry.description: A foreign key or logical relation between two tables.

from_table_id.description: Identity key of the source (referencing) table. Sort key 1.

to_table_id.description: Identity key of the target (referenced) table. Sort key 2.

constraint_name.description: Foreign key constraint name. Sort key 3.

from_columns.description: Ordered list of column identity keys in the source table.

to_columns.description: Ordered list of corresponding column identity keys in the target table.

MigrationLineage.title: MigrationLineage
MigrationLineage.description: Migration file discovery and schema evolution lineage.

migration_files.description: Discovered migration files, sorted by (datasource_id, version) ascending.

path.description: Workspace-relative path to the migration file.

datasource_id.description: Identity key of the associated data source, when determinable.

status.description: Migration status (if determinable from applied migrations table): 'applied' | 'pending' | 'unknown'.

applied_count.description: Number of migrations detected as applied. 0 when not determinable.

framework.description: Primary migration framework detected in the workspace.

QueryTouchpoint.title: QueryTouchpoint
QueryTouchpoint.description: A source code location that references a database table or column.

file.description: Workspace-relative source file path. Sort key 1.

table_id.description: Identity key of the referenced TableEntry.

column_ids.description: Identity keys of specific columns referenced, when determinable.

orm_method.description: ORM method name, when the touchpoint was detected through ORM usage (e.g., 'filter', 'where', 'find_by').
